page: Amazon Web Services g4dn.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores from an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 2.5 GHz. The system includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 system memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For hardware acceleration, the instance is equipped with four NVIDIA T4 GPUs based on the Turing architecture, delivering a combined 64 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided via a 900 GB NVMe SSD instance store, and network connectivity features a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance in parallelized and memory-intensive tasks. The instance achieves a multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 40,680.23 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10,986.0. In memory bandwidth testing, it registers 2,255.13 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, though uncached large-block reads drop to 108.42 GB/sec. Notably, the GPU configuration excels in LLM inference tasks; prompt processing speeds reach top-tier levels of 4,029.58 tokens/sec for a small 135M model, 1,006.4 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model, and 134.3 tokens/sec for a large 70B model. Text generation speeds are moderate, yielding 29.81 tokens/sec on the 7B model and 3.3 tokens/sec on the 70B model. Single-threaded tasks, such as gzip compression at 23.5 GB/sec, represent a relative performance bottleneck.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g4dn.12xlarge offers a balanced profile for multi-GPU workloads by bundling local NVMe storage and high-speed networking without requiring top-tier enterprise GPU pricing. The combination of four NVIDIA T4 GPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth makes this instance highly suitable for distributed machine learning inference, graphics rendering, video transcoding, and multi-threaded database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance optimized for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 48 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 2.5 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. The hardware configuration includes four NVIDIA T4 GPUs with a total of 64 GB VRAM, a 900 GB NVMe SSD local instance store, and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks demonstrate top-tier performance in LLM prompt processing, reaching 1,006.4 tokens/sec on 7B models, alongside solid multi-core CPU capabilities. However, single-threaded performance remains average. This instance provides a cost-effective multi-GPU platform for workloads such as machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and video transcoding.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, four NVIDIA T4 GPUs with 64 GB VRAM, and a 900 GB NVMe SSD for graphics and inference workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 48 Intel vCPUs, four NVIDIA T4 GPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A multi-GPU accelerated instance featuring four NVIDIA T4 GPUs, local NVMe storage, and high-speed networking for parallelized compute workloads.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA T4 GPUs providing 64 GB of total VRAM for graphics and machine learning.
Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 48 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of DDR4 system memory.
900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Top-tier LLM prompt processing performance across small, medium, and large models.
Optimized for machine learning inference, video transcoding, and graphics rendering.
